title: Commodities Brief — 11 Jun 2026
intro:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
11 June 2026 • 05:17 CET

COMMODITIES MARKETS — SESSION OVERVIEW

Commodities complex shows divergent momentum as precious metals retreat while energy gains traction. Dollar strength pressures metals across the board amid cooling safe-haven demand, though structural supercycle dynamics remain intact. Risk sentiment stabilizes following Tuesday's volatility.

PRECIOUS METALS

Gold

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
COMMODITIES BRIEF
11 June 2026 • 05:17 CET

COMMODITIES MARKETS — SESSION OVERVIEW

Commodities complex shows divergent momentum as precious metals retreat while energy gains traction. Dollar strength pressures metals across the board amid cooling safe-haven demand, though structural supercycle dynamics remain intact. Risk sentiment stabilizes following Tuesday's volatility.

PRECIOUS METALS

Gold trades at $4097.40/oz, down 0.26% intraday and 5.50% week-to-date, as real yields tick higher and central bank buying moderates. Silver underperforms at $63.86/oz, declining 1.14% on reduced industrial hedging ap

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
petite. Platinum falls 1.27% to $1666.50/oz amid automotive sector weakness, while palladium rallies 1.76% to $1252.50/oz on supply constraints from primary producers. Copper weakens to $6.22/lb, down 0.48%, signaling near-term growth concerns despite robust medium-term infrastructure demand.

ENERGY

Crude markets find support with WTI advancing 1.27% to $91.17/bbl and Brent gaining 1.08% to $94.11/bbl on stronger Asian demand indicators and OPEC-plus production discipline. The Brent-WTI spread holds steady at $2.94/bbl, reflecting balanced transatlantic arbitrage. Natural gas unchanged at $3.18/MMBtu as

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
seasonal storage builds continue.

AGRICULTURE

Grain complex shows limited movement. Wheat eases 0.55% to $584.25/bu on improved Black Sea export corridors. Corn trades at $417.25/bu, down 0.42%, while soybeans hold near-flat at $1122.50/bu supported by Chinese buying.

TRADE IDEAS

LONG: Palladium (supply deficit), WTI Crude (demand recovery), Soybeans (export strength)
SHORT/HEDGE: Gold (yield pressure), Silver (industrial slowdown), Copper (cyclical headwinds)

KEY RISKS

Dollar rally extending beyond current levels; unexpected OPEC-plus policy shift; adverse weather developments across U.S. Corn Belt.
